 {"id":"caam-h6a","title":"Implement 'env' command for shell integration","description":"## Task
Add 'caam env' command that prints environment variables for shell eval.

## Usage
```bash
eval "$(caam env codex work-1)"
# Now CODEX_HOME is set, can run codex directly

eval "$(caam env claude personal)"
# Now HOME and XDG_CONFIG_HOME are set
```

## Output Format
```bash
export CODEX_HOME="/home/user/.local/share/caam/profiles/codex/work-1/codex_home"
```

## Implementation
- Get provider env vars from Provider.Env()
- Format as shell export statements
- Support --unset to print unset commands

## Use Case
Power users who want to set up environment once and run multiple commands, rather than using 'caam exec' wrapper each time.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Outputs valid shell syntax
- Works with bash/zsh
- Can be eval'd without errors","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:35.006530391-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:23.66051241-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:23.66051241-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented caam env command with --unset and --fish flags for shell integration","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-h6a","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:35.020449197-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-hc7","title":"Implement 'use' and 'which' commands for default profiles","description":"## Task
Add commands to set and show default profiles per provider.

## `caam use` Command
```bash
caam use codex work-1     # Set work-1 as default for codex
caam use claude personal  # Set personal as default for claude
```

Sets the default profile in config. After this, commands that need a profile but don't specify one use the default.

## `caam which` Command
```bash
caam which           # Show defaults for all providers
caam which codex     # Show default for codex only
```

Output:
```
codex:  work-1
claude: personal
gemini: (none)
```

## Implementation
- Add DefaultProfiles map to config (already exists!)
- use: Update config and save
- which: Read config and display

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can set default profile per provider
- Can view defaults
- Commands that need profile use default when not specified","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:29.911282188-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:04.378795755-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:37:04.378795755-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 'caam use' and 'caam which' commands for setting and viewing default profiles per provider.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-hc7","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:54:29.932504866-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-hzu","title":"Add AuthModeDeviceCode to provider interface","description":"# Add AuthModeDeviceCode to Provider Interface

## What

Extend the provider.AuthMode enum to include device code flow as a first-class authentication method.

## Why

The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) is fundamentally different from browser-based OAuth:
- No localhost redirect needed
- User authenticates on a separate device
- CLI polls for token completion
- Better UX for headless environments

This deserves its own AuthMode rather than being a flag on OAuth because:
1. Different flow mechanics (polling vs redirect)
2. Different timeout characteristics
3. Different user interaction model
4. Profiles may be configured specifically for device-code-only scenarios

## Implementation Details

### File: internal/provider/provider.go

Add new constant:
\`\`\`go
const (
    AuthModeOAuth      AuthMode = "oauth"       // Browser-based OAuth flow
    AuthModeAPIKey     AuthMode = "api-key"     // API key authentication
    AuthModeDeviceCode AuthMode = "device-code" // OAuth device code flow (RFC 8628)
    AuthModeVertexADC  AuthMode = "vertex-adc"  // Vertex AI ADC
)
\`\`\`

### Interface Extension

Consider adding optional interface for providers that support device code:
\`\`\`go
// DeviceCodeProvider extends Provider with device code flow support
type DeviceCodeProvider interface {
    Provider
    // SupportsDeviceCode returns true if this provider supports device code flow
    SupportsDeviceCode() bool
    // LoginWithDeviceCode initiates device code authentication
    LoginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, p *profile.Profile) error
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AuthModeDeviceCode constant added
- [ ] DeviceCodeProvider interface defined (optional extension)
- [ ] Existing code continues to work (no breaking changes)
- [ ] Documentation comments explain when to use device-code vs oauth

## Testing

- Unit test that AuthModeDeviceCode is distinct from other modes
- Verify SupportedAuthModes() can include multiple modes

## Notes

- Keep backward compatible - existing profiles with "oauth" continue to work
- Profile.AuthMode field accepts the new value
- CLI will need to accept --device-code flag separately","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:22.865093603-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:41:58.077570631-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:41:58.077570631-05:00","close_reason":"Added AuthModeDeviceCode constant and DeviceCodeProvider interface to provider package."}
-{"id":"caam-i5j","title":"Signal handler for graceful operations","description":"## Purpose
Implement signal handling for SIGHUP-triggered reload and graceful shutdown.

## Background
Unix signals provide a standard way to communicate with running processes.
SIGHUP traditionally means "reload configuration".
SIGINT/SIGTERM mean "shutdown gracefully".

## Signals to Handle

| Signal | Action | Use Case |
|--------|--------|----------|
| SIGHUP | Reload profiles | External script triggered refresh |
| SIGINT | Graceful shutdown | Ctrl+C in terminal |
| SIGTERM | Graceful shutdown | Process manager (systemd, docker) |
| SIGUSR1 | Dump stats to log | Debugging/monitoring |

## Technical Implementation

### Signal Handler Package
Create internal/signals/signals.go with Handler struct that listens for
signals and exposes channels for reload, shutdown, and debug events.

### TUI Integration
Add watchSignals() tea.Cmd that listens to the signal handler channels
and emits appropriate messages (reloadRequestedMsg, tea.Quit, dumpStatsMsg).

### CLI for Sending Signals
Add `caam reload` command that finds TUI PID and sends SIGHUP.

### PID File Management
Write PID file on TUI start, remove on clean shutdown.

## Files to Create
- internal/signals/signals.go
- internal/signals/signals_test.go
- cmd/caam/reload.go

## Platform Considerations
- SIGHUP/SIGUSR1 not available on Windows
- Use build tags for platform-specific implementations
- Windows alternative: named pipe or file-based signaling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SIGHUP triggers profile reload in TUI
- [ ] SIGINT/SIGTERM cause graceful shutdown
- [ ] SIGUSR1 dumps stats to log file
- [ ] PID file created/removed correctly
- [ ] caam reload command works
- [ ] Graceful degradation on Windows","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:08.969830867-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:00:38.676451854-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-i5j","depends_on_id":"caam-0gx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:30.518098355-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"caam-i5j","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: implement internal/signals handler (SIGHUP reload, SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown, SIGUSR1 dump), integrate into TUI (reload + quit), manage PID file, and add caam reload command. Agent mail not configured; coordinating via bd comments.","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:00:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-ir7","title":"Project-profile association storage","description":"## Purpose
Create storage system for project-profile associations.

## Background
codex-pool pins conversations to accounts for context continuity.
For caam, we adapt this as "project-profile associations" - remembering which
profile a user prefers for each project directory.

This enables workflows like:
- Freelancers with multiple client accounts
- Work/personal separation by directory
- Team projects using team accounts

## Storage Format

### File Location
~/.caam/projects.json

### Schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "associations": {
    "/home/user/work/client-a": {
      "claude": "client-a@work.com",
      "codex": "work-main"
    },
    "/home/user/personal/blog": {
      "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
    }
  },
  "defaults": {
    "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
  }
}
```

## Technical Implementation

### Storage Interface
Create internal/project/store.go with ProjectStore struct that handles
loading, saving, and querying project associations.

### Path Matching
Support exact paths and glob patterns. Check parent directories if exact
match not found (inheritance).

### Atomic Writes
Use temp file + rename pattern for thread-safe updates (same pattern as
existing profile storage).

## Files to Create
- internal/project/store.go
- internal/project/store_test.go
- internal/project/matcher.go (path matching logic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Load and save project associations
- [ ] Query by directory path
- [ ] Parent directory inheritance works
- [ ] Atomic file writes
- [ ] Handle missing/corrupt file gracefully
- [ ] Version field for future migrations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:32.022502289-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:44.186281392-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:44.186281392-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented project-profile association storage (projects.json) with inheritance + glob matching, atomic writes, and tests.","comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"caam-ir7","author":"BlueBear","text":"Claimed by BlueBear. Implementing project association storage in internal/project (store + matcher + tests). No CLI wiring in this bead.","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:28:43Z"},{"id":3,"issue_id":"caam-ir7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes fix: project association store now canonicalizes provider keys/values on Load/Save (lowercase/trim, deterministic merge) to avoid nondeterministic Resolve and to ensure RemoveAssociation works with legacy/messy stored data. Added regression test.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:24:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ixy","title":"Detect and capture localhost redirect URLs from CLI output","description":"## Task
When running login commands (codex login, gemini), capture stdout and detect localhost URLs that need to be opened in a browser.

## Background
Codex CLI and Gemini CLI print a localhost URL during OAuth setup (e.g., http://localhost:1455/callback?code=...). Currently these auto-open in the default browser. We want to intercept and open in the configured browser profile instead.

## Implementation Approach
1. Wrap exec.Cmd with custom stdout handling
2. Use io.TeeReader or similar to both display output AND scan for URLs
3. Regex pattern: `http://localhost:[0-9]+[^ ]*`
4. When URL detected, suppress default browser open and use our launcher

## Challenges
- Some CLIs may use stderr for URLs
- Need to not break interactive features
- May need PTY for proper terminal handling
- Timing: URL may appear briefly before auto-open

## Alternative Approach
Set BROWSER env var to a wrapper script that calls back to caam. This is cleaner but requires a helper script.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can detect localhost URLs from codex login output
- Can detect localhost URLs from gemini output  
- Normal CLI output still visible to user
- Works with both stdout and stderr","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:31.574510149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:17:41.956736064-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:17:41.956736064-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented URL detection for OAuth flows: URLPattern regex, URLDetector io.Writer wrapper, OutputCapture for stdout/stderr, and BROWSER env var helper script generator.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ixy","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:31.58885101-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-j14","title":"Implement keyboard navigation and actions","description":"## Task
Wire up all keyboard shortcuts to their actions.

## Keybindings
- q: Quit
- ↑/↓: Navigate profiles
- Tab: Switch provider
- Enter: Run CLI with selected profile
- l: Login to selected profile
- o: Open account page in browser (requires browser epic)
- e: Edit profile
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter mode

## Implementation
- Handle key events in Update()
- Dispatch to appropriate actions
- Show confirmation dialogs for destructive actions

## Dependencies
- Requires browser epic for 'o' command
- Edit mode needs separate view state

## Acceptance Criteria
- All keybindings functional
- Delete requires confirmation
- Actions provide visual feedback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.395351148-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all keyboard navigation and actions: fixed 'l' key conflict, added Edit (e), Search/filter mode (/), updated help view and detail panel keybindings display","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.409454362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-ta7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.410828349-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-nmm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.411813644-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-jig","title":"Headless Authentication Support (Device Code Flow)","description":"# Headless Server Support

## Status: Core Features Complete!

The primary headless workflows are now fully functional:

### ✅ Completed

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Device auth setup | Works via native \`codex login --device-auth\` + \`caam backup\` |
| Hit-limit → switch → resume | Works via \`caam activate\` + native \`codex resume\` |
| Vault transfer | ✅ \`caam export/import\` implemented (caam-zha) |
| Documentation | ✅ AGENTS.md updated (caam-1r6) |
| AuthModeDeviceCode constant | ✅ Added (caam-hzu) |

### Remaining (P2/P3 - Profile Isolation Mode Only)

These are for the **advanced** Profile Isolation mode, not the primary workflow:

| ID | Task | Priority | Purpose |
|----|------|----------|---------|
| caam-v89 | --device-code CLI flag | P2 | For \`caam login\` in Profile Isolation mode |
| caam-ela | Codex provider support | P2 | Implementation for above |
| caam-0gy | Session resume | P3 | For Profile Isolation mode only |

## The Working Workflow

```bash
# SETUP (on headless server)
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam

# HIT LIMIT → SWITCH → RESUME
caam activate codex account2             # < 1 second!
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Native Codex
```

## Closed Tasks

| ID | Reason |
|----|--------|
| caam-1xv | Research complete - Claude lacks good headless option |
| caam-ai6 | Research complete - vault transfer is best for Gemini |
| caam-e79 | Removed - headless detection was over-engineering |
| caam-m5a | Removed - QR code was gold-plating |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:00.94446079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","close_reason":"Core headless workflows complete: device auth setup, hit-limit→switch→resume, vault transfer, and documentation. Remaining tasks (caam-v89, caam-ela) are P2 enhancements for Profile Isolation mode."}
-{"id":"caam-ljt","title":"Data retention and automatic cleanup","description":"## Purpose
Implement automatic cleanup of old activity logs to prevent unbounded database growth.

## Background
codex-pool uses configurable retention:
```go
logRetention: 7 * 24 * time.Hour, // Keep logs for 7 days
```

caam should have similar configurable retention with sensible defaults.

## Configuration
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
analytics:
  retention_days: 90        # Keep detailed logs for 90 days
  aggregate_retention: 365  # Keep aggregates for 1 year
  cleanup_on_startup: true  # Run cleanup when caam starts
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/db/cleanup.go

// CleanupConfig holds retention settings
type CleanupConfig struct {
    DetailedRetentionDays   int
    AggregateRetentionDays  int
}

// DefaultCleanupConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultCleanupConfig() CleanupConfig {
    return CleanupConfig{
        DetailedRetentionDays:  90,
        AggregateRetentionDays: 365,
    }
}

// Cleanup removes old records
func (d *DB) Cleanup(cfg CleanupConfig) (deleted int, err error) {
    cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -cfg.DetailedRetentionDays)
    
    result, err := d.conn.Exec(`
        DELETE FROM activity_log 
        WHERE timestamp < ?
    `, cutoff)
    
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    deleted, _ = result.RowsAffected()
    
    // Optionally VACUUM to reclaim space
    if deleted > 1000 {
        _, _ = d.conn.Exec("VACUUM")
    }
    
    return int(deleted), nil
}

// ScheduledCleanup runs cleanup periodically
func (d *DB) ScheduledCleanup(interval time.Duration, cfg CleanupConfig) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    for range ticker.C {
        d.Cleanup(cfg)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Manual cleanup
caam cleanup --dry-run
caam cleanup --older-than 30

# View database stats
caam db stats
Database: ~/.caam/data/caam.db
Size: 2.3 MB
Activity logs: 15,234 entries
Oldest entry: 2025-09-15
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/db/cleanup.go
- internal/db/cleanup_test.go
- cmd/caam/cleanup.go (optional CLI command)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Old logs are deleted based on retention config
- [ ] Cleanup is idempotent and safe
- [ ] VACUUM runs after large deletions
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows what would be deleted
- [ ] Cleanup does not block normal operations
- [ ] Sensible defaults that work for most users","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:38.014094581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:38.014094581-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-fwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:12.362276729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-0gb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:32:09.546260125-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"caam-ljt","title":"Data retention and automatic cleanup","description":"## Purpose
Implement automatic cleanup of old activity logs to prevent unbounded database growth.

## Background
codex-pool uses configurable retention:
```go
logRetention: 7 * 24 * time.Hour, // Keep logs for 7 days
```

caam should have similar configurable retention with sensible defaults.

## Configuration
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
analytics:
  retention_days: 90        # Keep detailed logs for 90 days
  aggregate_retention: 365  # Keep aggregates for 1 year
  cleanup_on_startup: true  # Run cleanup when caam starts
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/db/cleanup.go

// CleanupConfig holds retention settings
type CleanupConfig struct {
    DetailedRetentionDays   int
    AggregateRetentionDays  int
}

// DefaultCleanupConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultCleanupConfig() CleanupConfig {
    return CleanupConfig{
        DetailedRetentionDays:  90,
        AggregateRetentionDays: 365,
    }
}

// Cleanup removes old records
func (d *DB) Cleanup(cfg CleanupConfig) (deleted int, err error) {
    cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -cfg.DetailedRetentionDays)
    
    result, err := d.conn.Exec(`
        DELETE FROM activity_log 
        WHERE timestamp < ?
    `, cutoff)
    
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    deleted, _ = result.RowsAffected()
    
    // Optionally VACUUM to reclaim space
    if deleted > 1000 {
        _, _ = d.conn.Exec("VACUUM")
    }
    
    return int(deleted), nil
}

// ScheduledCleanup runs cleanup periodically
func (d *DB) ScheduledCleanup(interval time.Duration, cfg CleanupConfig) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    for range ticker.C {
        d.Cleanup(cfg)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Manual cleanup
caam cleanup --dry-run
caam cleanup --older-than 30

# View database stats
caam db stats
Database: ~/.caam/data/caam.db
Size: 2.3 MB
Activity logs: 15,234 entries
Oldest entry: 2025-09-15
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/db/cleanup.go
- internal/db/cleanup_test.go
- cmd/caam/cleanup.go (optional CLI command)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Old logs are deleted based on retention config
- [ ] Cleanup is idempotent and safe
- [ ] VACUUM runs after large deletions

 {"id":"caam-m5a","title":"Display QR code for device auth URL (nice-to-have)","description":"# Display QR Code for Device Auth URL

## What

When using device code flow, display a QR code that users can scan with their phone to quickly reach the authorization URL.

## Why

QR codes provide faster mobile access:
- No typing long URLs on phones
- Works even when URL can't be copy-pasted (some SSH clients)
- Professional/polished UX
- Many CLI tools (gh, az) do this successfully

## Implementation

### Dependencies

Use a pure-Go QR code library to avoid external dependencies:
- github.com/mdp/qrterminal (MIT license, popular)
- github.com/skip2/go-qrcode (generates image, not terminal)

### File: internal/auth/qrcode.go

\`\`\`go
package auth

import (
    "io"
    "os"
    
    "github.com/mdp/qrterminal/v3"
    "golang.org/x/term"
)

// DisplayQRCode prints a QR code to the terminal if stdout is a TTY
func DisplayQRCode(url string, w io.Writer) {
    // Only show QR if we have a TTY (not piped/redirected)
    if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) {
        return
    }
    
    // Use ASCII mode for compatibility
    config := qrterminal.Config{
        Level:     qrterminal.L,
        Writer:    w,
        BlackChar: qrterminal.WHITE, // Inverted for dark terminals
        WhiteChar: qrterminal.BLACK,
        QuietZone: 1,
    }
    qrterminal.GenerateWithConfig(url, config)
}
\`\`\`

### Integration in device code flow

\`\`\`go
func (p *Provider) loginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile) error {
    fmt.Println("Starting device code authentication...")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // For Codex, we need to capture the output to get the code
    // Then display our enhanced version
    
    // Display QR code pointing to the auth URL
    authURL := "https://auth.openai.com/codex/device"
    auth.DisplayQRCode(authURL, os.Stdout)
    
    fmt.Println("")
    fmt.Printf("Open: %s\\n", authURL)
    fmt.Println("Or scan the QR code above with your phone")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // Run the actual codex login --device-auth
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "codex", "login", "--device-auth")
    // ...
}
\`\`\`

## Output Example

\`\`\`
Starting device code authentication...

  █▀▀▀▀▀█ ▀▀▀▀█▀ █▀▀▀▀▀█
  █ ███ █ █▀▄█ ▀ █ ███ █
  █ ▀▀▀ █ ▀▄▀ ▄█ █ ▀▀▀ █
  ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀ ▀ █ ▀ ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀
  ██▀▀▀▀▀ ▀█▄ ▄▀▄▀█▄▀▄██
  ...

Open: https://auth.openai.com/codex/device
Or scan the QR code above with your phone

Enter code: ABCD-EFGH
\`\`\`

## UX Considerations

1. **Terminal compatibility**: QR codes may not render well in all terminals
   - Detect terminal capabilities if possible
   - Provide --no-qr flag to disable

2. **Color inversion**: Dark vs light terminals need different rendering
   - Try to auto-detect (COLORFGBG env var)
   - Provide --qr-invert flag

3. **Size**: QR code shouldn't overwhelm the terminal
   - Keep it reasonably sized
   - Use minimal quiet zone

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] QR code displays for device auth URL
- [ ] Works in common terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, GNOME Terminal)
- [ ] Gracefully skipped if not a TTY
- [ ] --no-qr flag to disable
- [ ] URL still printed as text (accessibility)

## Testing

- Visual test in various terminals
- Test with screen reader (ensure URL is still readable)
- Test when stdout is piped (QR should not appear)

## Notes

This is a polish/UX enhancement. Core device code flow should work without this.
Consider deferring until after core device code is working.

## Dependencies

- caam-ela: Codex device code implementation
- caam-v89: CLI --device-code flag","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:39:18.382441283-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","close_reason":"Removed as gold-plating. Codex already displays the URL clearly. QR code adds dependency and complexity for marginal UX gain. Keep caam simple.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-ela","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:18.087310678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-v89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:23.188174111-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-nac","title":"Implement browser launcher abstraction","description":"## Task
Create internal/browser/browser.go with a Launcher interface and implementations for different browsers.

## Design
```go
type Launcher interface {
    // Open opens a URL in the configured browser profile
    Open(url string) error
    // Name returns the browser name for display
    Name() string
}

type Config struct {
    Command    string // Browser executable
    ProfileDir string // Profile directory or name
}
```

## Implementations Needed
1. ChromeLauncher - uses --profile-directory flag
2. FirefoxLauncher - uses -P flag for profile name
3. DefaultLauncher - uses OS default (open/xdg-open)

## Platform Considerations
- macOS: Chrome is at /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
- Linux: Usually 'google-chrome' or 'chromium-browser'
- Windows: Complex paths, need registry lookup or common paths

## Rationale
Abstraction allows us to support multiple browsers and add new ones easily. The interface also makes testing straightforward.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launcher interface defined
- Chrome implementation works on macOS and Linux
- Firefox implementation works
- Default fallback works
- Unit tests for each implementation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.47883372-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented browser launcher abstraction with Launcher interface, ChromeLauncher (--profile-directory), FirefoxLauncher (-P), DefaultLauncher (xdg-open/open), and GenericLauncher. Includes platform-specific browser detection for macOS/Linux/Windows.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-nac","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.499689437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
